discoveryMethod: OSM/Overpass public listing research plus direct homepage fetches; duplicate checked against mockups.json, master-send-board.json, and prior daily batches

site HTTP 200; appointment/brake/tire terms; seven tel links

site HTTP 200; form/email/tel paths; storm language

discoveryCandidatesReviewed.businessName: Muscle Car Concepts and Restoration, LLC
discoveryCandidatesReviewed.primarySignal: site HTTP 200; appointment/schedule language; no tel link detected
public phone; no website field; no public email found

publicEvidence: Page title identifies Titze's Auto Repair | Sioux Falls, SD
First-page fetch found seven tel links
Homepage includes appointment, brake, tire, and schedule language
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: The business has a live site and appointment language, but a focused high-intent service lane is not evident from the first-page audit; repair-search visitors may still have to parse a broad homepage before calling or booking.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Test one narrow brake/tire or appointment landing page with a single mobile action, using only the service line the owner confirms.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: This is a contained optimization offer rather than a redesign claim; the site already has a working base and a reachable phone path.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup created; service priority and real shop assets need confirmation before a service-specific concept would be useful.

draftCopy.phoneScript: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was reviewing Sioux Falls auto-repair pages and saw Titze's site already has a working phone and appointment path. I had one narrower idea: a focused brake or tire service page with one clear mobile next step, instead of a generic redesign. Which service, if any, would be most useful to show as a sample? If it is not relevant, just say no and I will not follow up.

publicEvidence: Homepage text includes storm and emergency language
selectedLeads.contactRoute: official_site_forms_and_phone; no verified public email found
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: A broad roofing site with multiple forms can make storm-search traffic work harder than necessary; a single storm/inspection offer could be easier to track and act on.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Campaign-specific storm-damage inspection page with one tracked call/form action, after confirming the exact offer, coverage, and response expectations.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: The site already exists, so the conversation can stay focused on measurable campaign traffic rather than a full rebuild.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup today; a useful page requires the exact storm offer and approved photography/proof. Avoid generic storm imagery.

draftCopy.contactFormScript: Hi Nelson Contracting team — I came across your roofing site and noticed storm/emergency language is already present, so I would not lead with a generic redesign. A tighter opportunity may be a single storm-damage inspection page for search or paid traffic, with one call/form action and clearer measurement. I can sketch a no-obligation sample after confirming the offer and service area. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicEvidence: First-page fetch found one tel link and public Gmail address mikes66towing@gmail.com
selectedLeads.contactRoute: public Gmail plus official site phone path
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: A customer-facing homepage titled only Home gives search/social visitors little immediate context about towing or auto service, while the first-page audit surfaced only one tap-to-call path.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Clarify the first screen around the verified towing/auto-service route, repeat the phone action, and separate urgent calls from non-urgent service requests without inventing availability claims.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: The observation is concrete and helpful, the site is live, and a public email route exists; the offer can be a focused repair rather than a replacement-site pitch.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup created; exact service split and approved imagery are needed to avoid fabricating an emergency-dispatch promise.

email.subject: quick homepage clarity note for Mike's 66 & Towing
email.body:
Hi Mike's 66 & Towing team — I found your live site while checking Lincoln-area auto-service listings. The page loads, but the browser title is only “Home,” and the first-page audit surfaced just one clear tap-to-call path.

That can make it harder for a visitor to immediately understand the towing/auto-service route. I had a small, no-obligation idea for clarifying the first screen without claiming hours or response times that I have not verified. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

selectedLeads.businessName: Muscle Car Concepts and Restoration, LLC
publicEvidence: No tel link detected in first-page HTML audit
selectedLeads.contactRoute: official site appointment path and public phone; no verified business email found
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: A specialist restoration business has appointment language, but the first-page audit did not expose a tap-to-call link; high-consideration visitors may lack a fast way to start a project conversation on mobile.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: A restoration-project intake page with a visible phone action, project-photo upload direction, and expectations set by owner-approved wording.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Specialist work benefits from a more tailored intake than a generic repair page, and the offer can be scoped to lead quality rather than traffic promises.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup; a quality concept should use the shop's real project photos and confirmed intake process, not generic muscle-car imagery.

draftCopy.phoneScript: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was looking at Lincoln restoration businesses and noticed Muscle Car Concepts has appointment language, but I did not see a tap-to-call link in the first-page audit. I had an idea for a more specialist project-intake page using your real work photos and approved process. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicEvidence: OSM/Overpass listing identifies F & F Tire & Service
No website, email, or owned service page field found in this pass
selectedLeads.contactRoute: phone_only_found; no verified email or contact form
selectedLeads.nearestMoneyLeak: A local tire/service listing provides a phone but no controlled website path where a searcher can confirm services, hours, or request details before calling.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Profile cleanup plus a compact local service page, using only owner-confirmed tire/service categories, hours, photos, and call routing.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: The gap is easy to understand and the offer can start small; no expensive rebuild is implied.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup; only a listing phone was verified, so a page would be generic until service categories, hours, photos, and preferred contact process are confirmed.

draftCopy.phoneScript: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was reviewing local tire and auto-service listings and found F & F Tire & Service with a public phone listing, but no owned website field in the pass I checked. I had a small idea for a local service page/profile cleanup that would make the call path and confirmed services easier to understand. Who is the best person to send that idea to? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

qualityGateSummary: No new mockup met the quality bar today. Existing prior mockups were not reused or revised; all five selected records are research/draft only with revisionNeededBeforeOutreach=true.
